evals.prompt: Let's set up this new project
evals.expected_output: Should start the guided setup flow: ask about project name, target audience, tech stack, and offer to fill BRIEF.md

assertions.text: Asks about the project description or name
References BRIEF.md as a target file to fill
Does NOT skip directly to creative direction without covering the brief first

evals.prompt: Configure the project. BRIEF.md already has real content (no placeholders).
evals.expected_output: Should detect that BRIEF.md is already filled in and ask if the user wants to update specific sections instead of starting from scratch

assertions.text: Acknowledges that BRIEF.md is already filled in
Asks whether the user wants to update specific sections
Does NOT overwrite existing content without asking

evals.prompt: Set up the project. The project is not a git repository.
evals.expected_output: Should detect missing .git/ and ask if the user wants to initialize version control

assertions.text: Mentions that the project is not a git repository
Asks whether the user wants to initialize git

evals.prompt: I want to configure everything. There are 3 people on the team.
evals.expected_output: Should ask for team member names/GitHub accounts and add them to the Team list in CLAUDE.md

assertions.text: Asks for team member names or GitHub accounts
References the Team section in CLAUDE.md

evals.prompt: Set up the project. I'll skip most questions for now.
evals.expected_output: Should accept 'skip' gracefully without forcing answers, and show a summary at the end listing skipped sections

assertions.text: Accepts skip as a valid answer
Shows a summary with sections marked as skipped or to complete
